channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: NBA playoff guide: Who plays when, how to watch, what the odds are
item.description: It's go time. The race for the Larry O'Brien Trophy is finally beginning. The 2025 NBA playoffs start Saturday with four games: Indiana-Milwaukee, Denver-Los Angeles Clippers, New York-Detroit and Los Angeles Lakers-Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What injured ankle? Ja Morant flies around court helping Grizzlies take West's 8th seed
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Two-time All-Star Ja Morant promised a sprained right ankle wouldn't keep him out of Memphis' play-in game against Dallas with the Western Conference's last postseason berth up for grabs Friday night. Morant did more than just play.
item.dc:creator: CLAY BAILEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stars and Avs in playoff-opening series of Cup favorites that has Mikko Rantanen on a different side
item.description: FRISCO, Texas (AP) — Mikko Rantanen was at ease Friday when talking about the excitement of starting the playoffs, which the standout forward experienced with the Colorado Avalanche each of the past seven seasons that included a Stanley Cup title.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Warriors plan to combat Houston’s rough-and-tumble style in playoffs
item.description: Golden State Warriors look to say ‘composed and poised’ against gritty Rockets team with longtime Bay Area foes
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: Gregg Popovich has medical incident in restaurant, is resting at home, AP source says
item.description: San Antonio coach Gregg Popovich is doing well at home after needing medical attention earlier this week following an incident at a restaurant, a person with knowledge of the matter told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Yankees' Jazz Chisholm Jr. suspended 1 game following ejection, violation of social media policy
item.description: New York Yankees second baseman Jazz Chisholm Jr. was suspended for one game and fined by Major League Baseball on Friday following his ejection during a game at the Tampa Bay Rays and violation of MLB’s social media policy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: WWE legend Rey Mysterio confident in son's abilities as wrestling superstar
item.description: Rey Mysterio talked to Fox News Digital at WrestleMania After Dark at LIV Fontainebleau about his son, Dominik Mysterio, competing for the Intercontinental Championship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WWE legend Paul Heyman heaps praise on Paul Levesque ahead of Hall of Fame induction
item.description: WWE legend Paul Heyman told Fox News Digital in a recent interview that it was "long overdue" for Paul “Triple H” Levesque to enter the WWE Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boston Marathon and city insist all are welcome. But some runners say politics will keep them home
item.description: The Boston Marathon and Mayor Michelle Wu insist that international runners and other foreign visitors remain welcome in the city.
item.dc:creator: Jimmy Golen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bucks' Damian Lillard feeling grateful as he gets closer to joining his teammates in playoffs
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Milwaukee Bucks guard Damian Lillard isn’t sure exactly when he will be playing again. He’s just grateful that moment will come sooner than anyone could have reasonably expected.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White Sox look to stop 4-game slide, take on the Red Sox
item.description: Chicago White Sox (4-14, fifth in the AL Central) vs. Boston Red Sox (10-10, third in the AL East) Boston; Friday, 7:10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Kamala Harris paid LeBron James' entertainment company $50,000 for 'campaign event production,' records show
item.description: LeBron James endorsed Kamala Harris for president before the election, and the campaign paid his entertainment company $50,000 in January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dallas Stars going into playoffs without injured top goal scorer Jason Robertson
item.description: FRISCO, Texas (AP) — The Dallas Stars will begin the playoffs without top goal scorer Jason Robertson, who suffered a lower-body injury in their last regular-season game, coach Pete DeBoer said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Messi meets the Dawg Pound: Soccer superstar leads Inter Miami into Cleveland against Columbus
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Lionel Messi has played in his share of unique environments as one of the top soccer players in the world. He adds another on Saturday when the Argentine superstar visits the Dawg Pound.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PWHL chooses Vancouver as 1st expansion city with 2nd market to be determined, AP source says
item.description: The PWHL’s first expansion team will be based in Vancouver with an announcement scheduled for next week, a person with knowledge of the decision confirmed to The Associated Press on Friday. The person spoke on the condition of anonymity because the league has not revealed its plans.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pistons are back in the playoffs and take NBA-record postseason losing streak into Game 1 vs Knicks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York beating Detroit had become something of an NBA certainty, with the Knicks doing it 16 straight times over a span of five years. The Pistons finally ended that streak with three wins over the Knicks during their massive 2024-25 turnaround season.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dozens of 5th graders to complete 260-mile bike trek across California
item.description: Fifty-seven fifth graders rode their bikes across the state of California over seven days, testing their mental and physical strength and boosting their confidence and self-esteem.
item.dc:creator: Troy Hirsch, Fox 5

item.title: The NFL Draft is one week away; 49ers, Raiders eye top-valued picks
item.description: The 2025 NFL Draft begins on April 24 and the San Francisco 49ers and the Las Vegas Raiders both have a good shot at snagging top-10 valued talent.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KRON4

item.title: Karnišovas' vague end-of-year presser focuses on free agency, draft, and youth development
item.description: Artūras Karnišovas discussed the Chicago Bulls' end-of-year press conference, highlighting their goals of defining their style of play, retaining their 2025 first-round pick, targeting young players, and focusing on internal development, while also announcing their intention to pursue a premier free
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: IHSA upholds policy on transgender athletes, seeks clarity on state conflict with Trump order
item.description: The Illinois High School Association this week said it would keep in place its policy to allow transgender athletes to compete in high school sports competitions, despite an executive order from President Donald Trump, because the order may conflict with state law.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Report: TJ Edwards signs extension with the Bears
item.description: The Chicago Bears have signed linebacker TJ Edwards to a two-year, $20 million extension, with $16.6 million in guaranteed money, following the extension of defensive back Kyler Gordon.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Warriors-Rockets schedule for first-round playoff series
item.description: (KRON) -- They took the hard way in, but the Golden State Warriors are officially in the playoffs after securing a spot in Tuesday's nail-biting win over the Memphis Grizzlies. The No. 7 seed Dubs (48-34) will face the No. 2 seed Houston Rockets (52-30) in the first round.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Inside Jackalopes' new identity: Humpback Chubs
item.description: On Wednesdays this summer, the Jackalopes will be the Grand Junction Humpback Chubs. A new identity for Grand Junction’s baseball team is here.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Devon Dampier ready to take the reigns
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - As spring practice wraps up this week for the Utah football team, new quarterback Devon Dampier is getting more and more comfortable. It helps having played in new offensive coordinator Jason Beck's system the last two seasons at New Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Rowdies' Fernandes set to face brother in U.S. Open Cup
item.description: The Tampa Bay Rowdies' U.S. Open cup match against F-C Naples will be a family affair, as longtime Rowdies midfielder Leo Fernandes is set to face his younger brother, Gustavo.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Morales, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jackie Robinson Day and Breaking Barriers in downtown San Diego
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- April 15th annually celebrates Jackie Robinson Day around Major League Baseball. Also, with the San Diego Padres in tow and on board with 2025 Breaking Barriers.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Golden State Warriors warn fans of counterfeit playoff tickets
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors narrowly clinched a spot in the 2025 NBA Playoffs after a home win Tuesday night against the Memphis Grizzlies. Scammers, the team warned in a new advisory, are also looking to win big by selling fans counterfeit tickets.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, KRON4

item.title: Cubs bullpen works 5 shutout innings, Hoerner's go-ahead triple in 10th hands Padres first home loss, 2-1
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The San Diego Padres hadn't loss a home game yet on the young season. Until Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Hendricks plans to come back stronger than ever
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - This was supposed to be the season Taylor Hendricks took a big step forward. After showing promising signs his rookie year, in which he averaged 7.3 points in 40 games, Hendricks was excited to be a big part of the Jazz rebuild.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Warriors host Memphis Grizzlies in NBA play-in game Tuesday at Chase Center
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (BCN) -- The Golden State Warriors missed a chance to clinch a National Basketball Association playoff spot in their last regular season game on Sunday but will get another chance when they host a play-in game Tuesday against the Memphis Grizzlies at Chase Center in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: Crochet is still Crochet, even if Red now precedes 'Sox' on the front of his jersey
item.description: Garrett Crochet, the former White Sox pitcher, made his debut for the Boston Red Sox on Sunday, shutting down the Chicago White Sox with 11 strikeouts and one run. Despite the trade, Crochet has expressed his fondness for his former team and the memories he made on Chicago's South Side.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz close out season with 116-105 loss at Minnesota
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (ABC4 Sports) - The NBA season has mercifully come to an end for the Utah Jazz. The Jazz ended the worst season in franchise history with a 116-105 loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves, who were led by Anthony Edwards with 43 points.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Crow-Armstrong homers twice and Cubs take series with 4-2 victory over Dodgers
item.description: Pete Crow-Armstrong hit his first two home runs of the season, including a go-ahead solo shot in the seventh inning, Michael Busch homered against his former team again and the Chicago Cubs beat the Los Angeles Dodgers 4-2 on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 89th Masters Tournament
item.description: Roars for Rory. The famous sound of Sunday at the Masters Tournament could be for Rory McIlroy (-12) this year.
item.dc:creator: John Hart, WGN 9

item.title: Fan reaches into Mike Trout's glove to snatch a catch from Angels outfielder
item.description: A fan grabbed the ball out of Mike Trout's glove after the Los Angeles Angels star reached into the right field stands Saturday night to make what appeared to be a great catch against the Houston Astros
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rory McIlroy has masterful start at Augusta and closes in on career Grand Slam
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — The greatest start in the 91-year history of the Masters wasn't enough for Rory McIlroy. He delivered what looked to be a haymaker late Saturday afternoon, a 6-iron so majestic and pure that he walked 10 yards before he saw it land 6 feet away for eagle on the 15th hole.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kansas man who was falsely accused of being a shooter after Chiefs’ 2024 Super Bowl win has died
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — A Kansas man who was falsely accused of being among the shooters who opened fire at a rally celebrating the Kansas City Chiefs’ Super Bowl victory last year has died, his attorney said. Denton Loudermill Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
